title: Ambienttangential
summary: Formalizes equality modulo the defining function Q and the condition that a weighted ambient operator descends to boundary data.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Formalizes equality modulo the defining function Q and the condition that a weighted ambient operator descends to boundary data.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in Ambient/Ambienttangential.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Same Boundary Value. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def SameBoundaryValue (f g : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: Same Boundary Value def. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_def (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue f g ↔ CalConf.EqModQ f g

correspondence.leanType: theorem EqModQ_iff_exists_Qmul (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.EqModQ f g ↔ ∃ h : Function Conf, (fun U => f U - g U) = CalConf.Qmul h

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
